      42              : 
      43              : /*
      44              :  * If a catcache invalidation is processed while we are in the middle of
      45              :  * creating a catcache entry (or list), it might apply to the entry we're
      46              :  * creating, making it invalid before it's been inserted to the catcache.  To
      47              :  * catch such cases, we have a stack of "create-in-progress" entries.  Cache
      48              :  * invalidation marks any matching entries in the stack as dead, in addition
      49              :  * to the actual CatCTup and CatCList entries.
      50              :  */
      51              : typedef struct CatCInProgress
      52              : {
      53              :         CatCache   *cache;                      /* cache that the entry belongs to */
      54              :         uint32          hash_value;             /* hash of the entry; ignored for lists */
      55              :         bool            list;                   /* is it a list entry? */
      56              :         bool            dead;                   /* set when the entry is invalidated */
      57              :         struct CatCInProgress *next;
      58              : } CatCInProgress;
      59              : 
      60              : static CatCInProgress *catcache_in_progress_stack = NULL;
      61              : 
      62              :  /* #define CACHEDEBUG */       /* turns DEBUG elogs on */
      63              : 
      64              : /*
      65              :  * Given a hash value and the size of the hash table, find the bucket
      66              :  * in which the hash value belongs. Since the hash table must contain
      67              :  * a power-of-2 number of elements, this is a simple bitmask.
      68              :  */
      69              : #define HASH_INDEX(h, sz) ((Index) ((h) & ((sz) - 1)))
      70              :

     121              : /*
     122              :  *                                      internal support functions
     123              :  */
     124              : 
     125              : /*
     126              :  * Hash and equality functions for system types that are used as cache key
     127              :  * fields.  In some cases, we just call the regular SQL-callable functions for
     128              :  * the appropriate data type, but that tends to be a little slow, and the
     129              :  * speed of these functions is performance-critical.  Therefore, for data
     130              :  * types that frequently occur as catcache keys, we hard-code the logic here.
     131              :  * Avoiding the overhead of DirectFunctionCallN(...) is a substantial win, and
     132              :  * in certain cases (like int4) we can adopt a faster hash algorithm as well.
     133              :  */
     134              : 
     135              : static bool
     136         2587 : chareqfast(Datum a, Datum b)
     137              : {
     138         2587 :         return DatumGetChar(a) == DatumGetChar(b);
     139              : }
     140              : 
     141              : static uint32
     142         3989 : charhashfast(Datum datum)
     143              : {
     144         3989 :         return murmurhash32((int32) DatumGetChar(datum));
     145              : }
     146              : 
     147              : static bool
     148         8382 : nameeqfast(Datum a, Datum b)
     149              : {
     150         8382 :         char       *ca = NameStr(*DatumGetName(a));
     151         8382 :         char       *cb = NameStr(*DatumGetName(b));
     152              : 
     153         8382 :         return strncmp(ca, cb, NAMEDATALEN) == 0;
     154              : }
     155              : 
     156              : static uint32
     157        22475 : namehashfast(Datum datum)
     158              : {
     159        22475 :         char       *key = NameStr(*DatumGetName(datum));
     160              : 
     161        22475 :         return hash_any((unsigned char *) key, strlen(key));
     162              : }
     163              : 
     164              : static bool
     165         4171 : int2eqfast(Datum a, Datum b)
     166              : {
     167         4171 :         return DatumGetInt16(a) == DatumGetInt16(b);
     168              : }
     169              : 
     170              : static uint32
     171        12755 : int2hashfast(Datum datum)
     172              : {
     173        12755 :         return murmurhash32((int32) DatumGetInt16(datum));

     177        93550 : int4eqfast(Datum a, Datum b)

     467              : /*
     468              :  *              CatCacheRemoveCTup
     469              :  *
     470              :  * Unlink and delete the given cache entry
     471              :  *
     472              :  * NB: if it is a member of a CatCList, the CatCList is deleted too.
     473              :  * Both the cache entry and the list had better have zero refcount.
     474              :  */
     475              : static void
     476         5153 : CatCacheRemoveCTup(CatCache *cache, CatCTup *ct)
     477              : {
     478              :         Assert(ct->refcount == 0);
     479              :         Assert(ct->my_cache == cache);
     480              : 
     481         5153 :         if (ct->c_list)
     482              :         {
     483              :                 /*
     484              :                  * The cleanest way to handle this is to call CatCacheRemoveCList,
     485              :                  * which will recurse back to me, and the recursive call will do the
     486              :                  * work.  Set the "dead" flag to make sure it does recurse.
     487              :                  */
     488            0 :                 ct->dead = true;
     489            0 :                 CatCacheRemoveCList(cache, ct->c_list);
     490            0 :                 return;                                 /* nothing left to do */
     491              :         }
     492              : 
     493              :         /* delink from linked list */
     494         5153 :         dlist_delete(&ct->cache_elem);
     495              : 
     496              :         /*
     497              :          * Free keys when we're dealing with a negative entry, normal entries just
     498              :          * point into tuple, allocated together with the CatCTup.
     499              :          */
     500         5153 :         if (ct->negative)
     501         1750 :                 CatCacheFreeKeys(cache->cc_tupdesc, cache->cc_nkeys,
     502         1750 :                                                  cache->cc_keyno, ct->keys);
     503              : 
     504         5153 :         pfree(ct);
     505              : 
     506         5153 :         --cache->cc_ntup;
     507         5153 :         --CacheHdr->ch_ntup;
     508              : }
     509              : 
     510              : /*
     511              :  *              CatCacheRemoveCList
     512              :  *
     513              :  * Unlink and delete the given cache list entry
     514              :  *
     515              :  * NB: any dead member entries that become unreferenced are deleted too.
     516              :  */
     517              : static void
     518          264 : CatCacheRemoveCList(CatCache *cache, CatCList *cl)
     519              : {
     520              :         int                     i;
     521              : 
     522              :         Assert(cl->refcount == 0);
     523              :         Assert(cl->my_cache == cache);
     524              : 
     525              :         /* delink from member tuples */
     526          756 :         for (i = cl->n_members; --i >= 0;)
     527              :         {
     528          492 :                 CatCTup    *ct = cl->members[i];
     529              : 
     530              :                 Assert(ct->c_list == cl);
     531          492 :                 ct->c_list = NULL;
     532              :                 /* if the member is dead and now has no references, remove it */
     533          492 :                 if (
     534              : #ifndef CATCACHE_FORCE_RELEASE
     535          492 :                         ct->dead &&
     536              : #endif
     537            0 :                         ct->refcount == 0)
     538            0 :                         CatCacheRemoveCTup(cache, ct);
     539              :         }
     540              : 
     541              :         /* delink from linked list */
     542          264 :         dlist_delete(&cl->cache_elem);
     543              : 
     544              :         /* free associated column data */
     545          264 :         CatCacheFreeKeys(cache->cc_tupdesc, cl->nkeys,
     546          264 :                                          cache->cc_keyno, cl->keys);
     547              : 
     548          264 :         pfree(cl);
     549          264 : }
     550              : 
     551              : 
     552              : /*
     553              :  *      CatCacheInvalidate
     554              :  *
     555              :  *      Invalidate entries in the specified cache, given a hash value.
     556              :  *
     557              :  *      We delete cache entries that match the hash value, whether positive
     558              :  *      or negative.  We don't care whether the invalidation is the result
     559              :  *      of a tuple insertion or a deletion.
     560              :  *
     561              :  *      We used to try to match positive cache entries by TID, but that is
     562              :  *      unsafe after a VACUUM FULL on a system catalog: an inval event could
     563              :  *      be queued before VACUUM FULL, and then processed afterwards, when the
     564              :  *      target tuple that has to be invalidated has a different TID than it
     565              :  *      did when the event was created.  So now we just compare hash values and
     566              :  *      accept the small risk of unnecessary invalidations due to false matches.
     567              :  *
     568              :  *      This routine is only quasi-public: it should only be used by inval.c.
     569              :  */
     570              : void
     571        38577 : CatCacheInvalidate(CatCache *cache, uint32 hashValue)
     572              : {
     573              :         Index           hashIndex;
     574              :         dlist_mutable_iter iter;
     575              : 
     576              :         CACHE_elog(DEBUG2, "CatCacheInvalidate: called");
     577              : 
     578              :         /*
     579              :          * We don't bother to check whether the cache has finished initialization
     580              :          * yet; if not, there will be no entries in it so no problem.
     581              :          */
     582              : 
     583              :         /*
     584              :          * Invalidate *all* CatCLists in this cache; it's too hard to tell which
     585              :          * searches might still be correct, so just zap 'em all.
     586              :          */
     587        38841 :         dlist_foreach_modify(iter, &cache->cc_lists)
     588              :         {
     589          264 :                 CatCList   *cl = dlist_container(CatCList, cache_elem, iter.cur);
     590              : 
     591          264 :                 if (cl->refcount > 0)
     592            0 :                         cl->dead = true;
     593              :                 else
     594          264 :                         CatCacheRemoveCList(cache, cl);
     595              :         }
     596              : 
     597              :         /*
     598              :          * inspect the proper hash bucket for tuple matches
     599              :          */
     600        38577 :         hashIndex = HASH_INDEX(hashValue, cache->cc_nbuckets);
     601        66188 :         dlist_foreach_modify(iter, &cache->cc_bucket[hashIndex])
     602              :         {
     603        27611 :                 CatCTup    *ct = dlist_container(CatCTup, cache_elem, iter.cur);
     604              : 
     605        27611 :                 if (hashValue == ct->hash_value)
     606              :                 {
     607         5153 :                         if (ct->refcount > 0 ||
     608         5146 :                                 (ct->c_list && ct->c_list->refcount > 0))
     609              :                         {
     610            7 :                                 ct->dead = true;
     611              :                                 /* list, if any, was marked dead above */
     612            7 :                                 Assert(ct->c_list == NULL || ct->c_list->dead);
     613              :                         }
     614              :                         else
     615         5146 :                                 CatCacheRemoveCTup(cache, ct);
     616              :                         CACHE_elog(DEBUG2, "CatCacheInvalidate: invalidated");
     617              : #ifdef CATCACHE_STATS
     618              :                         cache->cc_invals++;
     619              : #endif
     620              :                         /* could be multiple matches, so keep looking! */
     621              :                 }
     622              :         }
     623              : 
     624              :         /* Also invalidate any entries that are being built */
     625        38577 :         for (CatCInProgress *e = catcache_in_progress_stack; e != NULL; e = e->next)
     626              :         {
     627            0 :                 if (e->cache == cache)
     628              :                 {
     629            0 :                         if (e->list || e->hash_value == hashValue)
     630            0 :                                 e->dead = true;
     631              :                 }
     632              :         }
     633        38577 : }

     635              : /* ----------------------------------------------------------------
     636              :  *                                         public functions

     640              : 
     641              : /*
     642              :  * Standard routine for creating cache context if it doesn't exist yet
     643              :  *
     644              :  * There are a lot of places (probably far more than necessary) that check
     645              :  * whether CacheMemoryContext exists yet and want to create it if not.
     646              :  * We centralize knowledge of exactly how to create it here.
     647              :  */

     649           13 : CreateCacheMemoryContext(void)
     650              : {
     651              :         /*
     652              :          * Purely for paranoia, check that context doesn't exist; caller probably
     653              :          * did so already.
     654              :          */
     655           13 :         if (!CacheMemoryContext)
     656           13 :                 CacheMemoryContext = AllocSetContextCreate(TopMemoryContext,
     657              :                                                                                                    "CacheMemoryContext",
     658              :                                                                                                    ALLOCSET_DEFAULT_SIZES);
     659           13 : }

     661              : 
     662              : /*
     663              :  *              ResetCatalogCache
     664              :  *
     665              :  * Reset one catalog cache to empty.
     666              :  *
     667              :  * This is not very efficient if the target cache is nearly empty.
     668              :  * However, it shouldn't need to be efficient; we don't invoke it often.
     669              :  *
     670              :  * If 'debug_discard' is true, we are being called as part of
     671              :  * debug_discard_caches.  In that case, the cache is not reset for
     672              :  * correctness, but just to get more testing of cache invalidation.  We skip
     673              :  * resetting in-progress build entries in that case, or we'd never make any
     674              :  * progress.
     675              :  */

     677          156 : ResetCatalogCache(CatCache *cache, bool debug_discard)

     680              :         int                     i;

     682              :         /* Remove each list in this cache, or at least mark it dead */

     684              :         {
     685            0 :                 CatCList   *cl = dlist_container(CatCList, cache_elem, iter.cur);
     686              : 
     687            0 :                 if (cl->refcount > 0)
     688            0 :                         cl->dead = true;
     689              :                 else
     690            0 :                         CatCacheRemoveCList(cache, cl);
     691              :         }
     692              : 
     693              :         /* Remove each tuple in this cache, or at least mark it dead */

     698         4604 :                 dlist_foreach_modify(iter, bucket)
     699              :                 {
     700            0 :                         CatCTup    *ct = dlist_container(CatCTup, cache_elem, iter.cur);

     702            0 :                         if (ct->refcount > 0 ||
     703            0 :                                 (ct->c_list && ct->c_list->refcount > 0))
     704              :                         {
     705            0 :                                 ct->dead = true;
     706              :                                 /* list, if any, was marked dead above */
     707            0 :                                 Assert(ct->c_list == NULL || ct->c_list->dead);
     708              :                         }
     709              :                         else
     710            0 :                                 CatCacheRemoveCTup(cache, ct);
     711              : #ifdef CATCACHE_STATS
     712              :                         cache->cc_invals++;
     713              : #endif
     714              :                 }
     715              :         }
     716              : 
     717              :         /* Also invalidate any entries that are being built */
     718          156 :         if (!debug_discard)
     719              :         {
     720          156 :                 for (CatCInProgress *e = catcache_in_progress_stack; e != NULL; e = e->next)

     724              :                 }
     725              :         }
     726          156 : }

     728              : /*
     729              :  *              ResetCatalogCaches

     740            2 : ResetCatalogCachesExt(bool debug_discard)

     755              : 
     756              : /*
     757              :  *              CatalogCacheFlushCatalog
     758              :  *
     759              :  *      Flush all catcache entries that came from the specified system catalog.
     760              :  *      This is needed after VACUUM FULL/CLUSTER on the catalog, since the
     761              :  *      tuples very likely now have different TIDs than before.  (At one point
     762              :  *      we also tried to force re-execution of CatalogCacheInitializeCache for
     763              :  *      the cache(s) on that catalog.  This is a bad idea since it leads to all
     764              :  *      kinds of trouble if a cache flush occurs while loading cache entries.
     765              :  *      We now avoid the need to do it by copying cc_tupdesc out of the relcache,
     766              :  *      rather than relying on the relcache to keep a tupdesc for us.  Of course
     767              :  *      this assumes the tupdesc of a cachable system table will not change...)
     768              :  */
     769              : void
     770            0 : CatalogCacheFlushCatalog(Oid catId)
     771              : {
     772              :         slist_iter      iter;
     773              : 
     774              :         CACHE_elog(DEBUG2, "CatalogCacheFlushCatalog called for %u", catId);
     775              : 
     776            0 :         slist_foreach(iter, &CacheHdr->ch_caches)
     777              :         {
     778            0 :                 CatCache   *cache = slist_container(CatCache, cc_next, iter.cur);
     779              : 
     780              :                 /* Does this cache store tuples of the target catalog? */
     781            0 :                 if (cache->cc_reloid == catId)
     782              :                 {
     783              :                         /* Yes, so flush all its contents */
     784            0 :                         ResetCatalogCache(cache, false);
     785              : 
     786              :                         /* Tell inval.c to call syscache callbacks for this cache */
     787            0 :                         CallSyscacheCallbacks(cache->id, 0);
     788              :                 }
     789              :         }
     790              : 
     791              :         CACHE_elog(DEBUG2, "end of CatalogCacheFlushCatalog call");
     792            0 : }

     794              : /*
     795              :  *              InitCatCache
     796              :  *
     797              :  *      This allocates and initializes a cache for a system catalog relation.
     798              :  *      Actually, the cache is only partially initialized to avoid opening the
     799              :  *      relation.  The relation will be opened and the rest of the cache
     800              :  *      structure initialized on the first access.
     801              :  */

     803              : #define InitCatCache_DEBUG2 \
     804              : do { \
     805              :         elog(DEBUG2, "InitCatCache: rel=%u ind=%u id=%d nkeys=%d size=%d", \
     806              :                  cp->cc_reloid, cp->cc_indexoid, cp->id, \
     807              :                  cp->cc_nkeys, cp->cc_nbuckets); \
     808              : } while(0)

     810              : #define InitCatCache_DEBUG2
     811              : #endif

     813              : CatCache *
     814         1014 : InitCatCache(int id,
     815              :                          Oid reloid,
     816              :                          Oid indexoid,
     817              :                          int nkeys,
     818              :                          const int *key,
     819              :                          int nbuckets)
     820              : {
     821              :         CatCache   *cp;
     822              :         MemoryContext oldcxt;
     823              :         size_t          sz;
     824              :         int                     i;
     825              : 
     826              :         /*
     827              :          * nbuckets is the initial number of hash buckets to use in this catcache.
     828              :          * It will be enlarged later if it becomes too full.
     829              :          *
     830              :          * nbuckets must be a power of two.  We check this via Assert rather than
     831              :          * a full runtime check because the values will be coming from constant
     832              :          * tables.
     833              :          *
     834              :          * If you're confused by the power-of-two check, see comments in
     835              :          * bitmapset.c for an explanation.
     836              :          */
     837              :         Assert(nbuckets > 0 && (nbuckets & -nbuckets) == nbuckets);
     838              : 
     839              :         /*
     840              :          * first switch to the cache context so our allocations do not vanish at
     841              :          * the end of a transaction
     842              :          */
     843         1014 :         if (!CacheMemoryContext)
     844            0 :                 CreateCacheMemoryContext();
     845              : 
     846         1014 :         oldcxt = MemoryContextSwitchTo(CacheMemoryContext);
     847              : 
     848              :         /*
     849              :          * if first time through, initialize the cache group header
     850              :          */
     851         1014 :         if (CacheHdr == NULL)
     852              :         {
     853           13 :                 CacheHdr = (CatCacheHeader *) palloc(sizeof(CatCacheHeader));
     854           13 :                 slist_init(&CacheHdr->ch_caches);
     855           13 :                 CacheHdr->ch_ntup = 0;
     856              : #ifdef CATCACHE_STATS
     857              :                 /* set up to dump stats at backend exit */
     858              :                 on_proc_exit(CatCachePrintStats, 0);
     859              : #endif
     860              :         }
     861              : 
     862              :         /*
     863              :          * Allocate a new cache structure, aligning to a cacheline boundary
     864              :          *
     865              :          * Note: we rely on zeroing to initialize all the dlist headers correctly
     866              :          */
     867         1014 :         sz = sizeof(CatCache) + PG_CACHE_LINE_SIZE;
     868         1014 :         cp = (CatCache *) CACHELINEALIGN(palloc0(sz));
     869         1014 :         cp->cc_bucket = palloc0(nbuckets * sizeof(dlist_head));
     870              : 
     871              :         /*
     872              :          * initialize the cache's relation information for the relation
     873              :          * corresponding to this cache, and initialize some of the new cache's
     874              :          * other internal fields.  But don't open the relation yet.
     875              :          */
     876         1014 :         cp->id = id;
     877         1014 :         cp->cc_relname = "(not known yet)";
     878         1014 :         cp->cc_reloid = reloid;
     879         1014 :         cp->cc_indexoid = indexoid;
     880         1014 :         cp->cc_relisshared = false; /* temporary */
     881         1014 :         cp->cc_tupdesc = (TupleDesc) NULL;
     882         1014 :         cp->cc_ntup = 0;
     883         1014 :         cp->cc_nbuckets = nbuckets;
     884         1014 :         cp->cc_nkeys = nkeys;
     885         2652 :         for (i = 0; i < nkeys; ++i)
     886         1638 :                 cp->cc_keyno[i] = key[i];
     887              : 
     888              :         /*
     889              :          * new cache is initialized as far as we can go for now. print some
     890              :          * debugging information, if appropriate.
     891              :          */
     892              :         InitCatCache_DEBUG2;
     893              : 
     894              :         /*
     895              :          * add completed cache to top of group header's list
     896              :          */
     897         1014 :         slist_push_head(&CacheHdr->ch_caches, &cp->cc_next);
     898              : 
     899              :         /*
     900              :          * back to the old context before we return...
     901              :          */
     902         1014 :         MemoryContextSwitchTo(oldcxt);
     903              : 
     904         1014 :         return cp;
     905              : }

     907              : /*
     908              :  * Enlarge a catcache, doubling the number of buckets.
     909              :  */
     910              : static void
     911           20 : RehashCatCache(CatCache *cp)

     914              :         int                     newnbuckets;
     915              :         int                     i;
     916              : 
     917           20 :         elog(DEBUG1, "rehashing catalog cache id %d for %s; %d tups, %d buckets",
     918              :                  cp->id, cp->cc_relname, cp->cc_ntup, cp->cc_nbuckets);
     919              : 
     920              :         /* Allocate a new, larger, hash table. */
     921           20 :         newnbuckets = cp->cc_nbuckets * 2;
     922           20 :         newbucket = (dlist_head *) MemoryContextAllocZero(CacheMemoryContext, newnbuckets * sizeof(dlist_head));

     924              :         /* Move all entries from old hash table to new. */

     942           20 :         cp->cc_bucket = newbucket;
     943           20 : }

     945              : /*
     946              :  *              CatalogCacheInitializeCache

     961              :                                 i+1, cache->cc_nkeys, cache->cc_keyno[i], \

     990              : 
     991          412 :         oldcxt = MemoryContextSwitchTo(CacheMemoryContext);

    1005              :         /*

    1009              :

    1011              :

    1029              : 
    1030          662 :                         keytype = attr->atttypid;

    1032              :                         Assert(attr->attnotnull);
    1033              :                 }

    1035              :                 {
    1036            0 :                         if (cache->cc_keyno[i] < 0)

    1040              : 
    1041          662 :                 GetCCHashEqFuncs(keytype,

    1073              : /*
    1074              :  * InitCatCachePhase2 -- external interface for CatalogCacheInitializeCache
    1075              :  *
    1076              :  * One reason to call this routine is to ensure that the relcache has
    1077              :  * created entries for all the catalogs and indexes referenced by catcaches.
    1078              :  * Therefore, provide an option to open the index as well as fixing the
    1079              :  * cache itself.  An exception is the indexes on pg_am, which we don't use
    1080              :  * (cf. IndexScanOK).
    1081              :  */
    1082              : void
    1083          322 : InitCatCachePhase2(CatCache *cache, bool touch_index)
    1084              : {
    1085          322 :         if (cache->cc_tupdesc == NULL)
    1086          296 :                 CatalogCacheInitializeCache(cache);
    1087              :

    1092              :                 Relation        idesc;

    1094              :                 /*

    1117              : 
    1118              : /*
    1119              :  *              IndexScanOK
    1120              :  *
    1121              :  *              This function checks for tuples that will be fetched by
    1122              :  *              IndexSupportInitialize() during relcache initialization for
    1123              :  *              certain system indexes that support critical syscaches.
    1124              :  *              We can't use an indexscan to fetch these, else we'll get into
    1125              :  *              infinite recursion.  A plain heap scan will work, however.
    1126              :  *              Once we have completed relcache initialization (signaled by
    1127              :  *              criticalRelcachesBuilt), we don't have to worry anymore.
    1128              :  *
    1129              :  *              Similarly, during backend startup we have to be able to use the
    1130              :  *              pg_authid and pg_auth_members syscaches for authentication even if
    1131              :  *              we don't yet have relcache entries for those catalogs' indexes.
    1132              :  */
    1133              : static bool
    1134        11228 : IndexScanOK(CatCache *cache, ScanKey cur_skey)
    1135              : {
    1136        11228 :         switch (cache->id)
    1137              :         {
    1138          812 :                 case INDEXRELID:
    1139              : 
    1140              :                         /*
    1141              :                          * Rather than tracking exactly which indexes have to be loaded
    1142              :                          * before we can use indexscans (which changes from time to time),
    1143              :                          * just force all pg_index searches to be heap scans until we've
    1144              :                          * built the critical relcaches.
    1145              :                          */
    1146          812 :                         if (!criticalRelcachesBuilt)
    1147          178 :                                 return false;
    1148          634 :                         break;
    1149              : 
    1150           24 :                 case AMOID:
    1151              :                 case AMNAME:
    1152              : 
    1153              :                         /*
    1154              :                          * Always do heap scans in pg_am, because it's so small there's
    1155              :                          * not much point in an indexscan anyway.  We *must* do this when
    1156              :                          * initially building critical relcache entries, but we might as
    1157              :                          * well just always do it.
    1158              :                          */
    1159           24 :                         return false;
    1160              : 
    1161           44 :                 case AUTHNAME:
    1162              :                 case AUTHOID:
    1163              :                 case AUTHMEMMEMROLE:
    1164              : 
    1165              :                         /*
    1166              :                          * Protect authentication lookups occurring before relcache has
    1167              :                          * collected entries for shared indexes.
    1168              :                          */
    1169           44 :                         if (!criticalSharedRelcachesBuilt)
    1170            4 :                                 return false;
    1171           40 :                         break;
    1172              : 
    1173        10348 :                 default:
    1174        10348 :                         break;
    1175              :         }
    1176              : 
    1177              :         /* Normal case, allow index scan */
    1178        11022 :         return true;
    1179              : }

    1181              : /*
    1182              :  *      SearchCatCacheInternal
    1183              :  *
    1184              :  *              This call searches a system cache for a tuple, opening the relation
    1185              :  *              if necessary (on the first access to a particular cache).
    1186              :  *
    1187              :  *              The result is NULL if not found, or a pointer to a HeapTuple in
    1188              :  *              the cache.  The caller must not modify the tuple, and must call
    1189              :  *              ReleaseCatCache() when done with it.
    1190              :  *
    1191              :  * The search key values should be expressed as Datums of the key columns'
    1192              :  * datatype(s).  (Pass zeroes for any unused parameters.)  As a special
    1193              :  * exception, the passed-in key for a NAME column can be just a C string;
    1194              :  * the caller need not go to the trouble of converting it to a fully
    1195              :  * null-padded NAME.
    1196              :  */

    1207              :

    1356              :  * This is kept separate from SearchCatCacheInternal() to keep the fast-path

    1383              : 
    1384              :         /*
    1385              :          * Tuple was not found in cache, so we have to try to retrieve it directly

    1387              :          * found, we will add a negative cache entry instead.
    1388              :          *
    1389              :          * NOTE: it is possible for recursive cache lookups to occur while reading
    1390              :          * the relation --- for example, due to shared-cache-inval messages being
    1391              :          * processed during table_open().  This is OK.  It's even possible for one
    1392              :          * of those lookups to find and enter the very same tuple we are trying to
    1393              :          * fetch here.  If that happens, we will enter a second copy of the tuple
    1394              :          * into the cache.  The first copy will never be referenced again, and
    1395              :          * will eventually age out of the cache, so there's no functional problem.
    1396              :          * This case is rare enough that it's not worth expending extra cycles to
    1397              :          * detect.
    1398              :          *
    1399              :          * Another case, which we *must* handle, is that the tuple could become
    1400              :          * outdated during CatalogCacheCreateEntry's attempt to detoast it (since
    1401              :          * AcceptInvalidationMessages can run during TOAST table access).  We do
    1402              :          * not want to return already-stale catcache entries, so we loop around
    1403              :          * and do the table scan again if that happens.
    1404              :          */
    1405        10811 :         relation = table_open(cache->cc_reloid, AccessShareLock);

    1407              :         do
    1408              :         {
    1409              :                 /*
    1410              :                  * Ok, need to make a lookup in the relation, copy the scankey and

    1412              :                  * because systable_beginscan scribbles on the scankey.)
    1413              :                  */
    1414        10811 :                 memcpy(cur_skey, cache->cc_skey, sizeof(ScanKeyData) * nkeys);

    1416        10811 :                 cur_skey[1].sk_argument = v2;
    1417        10811 :                 cur_skey[2].sk_argument = v3;
    1418        10811 :                 cur_skey[3].sk_argument = v4;
    1419              : 
    1420        10811 :                 scandesc = systable_beginscan(relation,
    1421              :                                                                           cache->cc_indexoid,
    1422        10811 :                                                                           IndexScanOK(cache, cur_skey),
    1423              :                                                                           NULL,
    1424              :                                                                           nkeys,
    1425              :                                                                           cur_skey);
    1426              : 
    1427        10811 :                 ct = NULL;
    1428        10811 :                 stale = false;
    1429              : 
    1430        10811 :                 while (HeapTupleIsValid(ntp = systable_getnext(scandesc)))
    1431              :                 {
    1432         6579 :                         ct = CatalogCacheCreateEntry(cache, ntp, NULL,
    1433              :                                                                                  hashValue, hashIndex);
    1434              :                         /* upon failure, we must start the scan over */
    1435         6579 :                         if (ct == NULL)
    1436              :                         {
    1437            0 :                                 stale = true;
    1438            0 :                                 break;
    1439              :                         }
    1440              :                         /* immediately set the refcount to 1 */
    1441         6579 :                         ResourceOwnerEnlargeCatCacheRefs(CurrentResourceOwner);
    1442         6579 :                         ct->refcount++;
    1443         6579 :                         ResourceOwnerRememberCatCacheRef(CurrentResourceOwner, &ct->tuple);
    1444         6579 :                         break;                          /* assume only one match */
    1445              :                 }
    1446              : 
    1447        10811 :                 systable_endscan(scandesc);
    1448        10811 :         } while (stale);
    1449              : 
    1450        10811 :         table_close(relation, AccessShareLock);
    1451              : 
    1452              :         /*

    1498              : /*
    1499              :  *      ReleaseCatCache

    1504              :  *      NOTE: if compiled with -DCATCACHE_FORCE_RELEASE then catcache entries
    1505              :  *      will be freed as soon as their refcount goes to zero.  In combination

    1561              : /*
    1562              :  *      SearchCatCacheList
    1563              :  *
    1564              :  *              Generate a list of all tuples matching a partial key (that is,

    1566              :  *

    1574              :  */

    1597              :

    1604              :         Assert(nkeys > 0 && nkeys < cache->cc_nkeys);
    1605              :

    1609              : 
    1610              :         /* Initialize local parameter array */

    1612         2201 :         arguments[1] = v2;

    1614         2201 :         arguments[3] = v4;
    1615              : 
    1616              :         /*
    1617              :          * compute a hash value of the given keys for faster search.  We don't

    1621         2201 :         lHashValue = CatalogCacheComputeHashValue(cache, nkeys, v1, v2, v3, v4);
    1622              : 
    1623              :         /*

    1634            0 :                         continue;                       /* ignore dead entries */
    1635              : 
    1636        12720 :                 if (cl->hash_value != lHashValue)
    1637        10936 :                         continue;                       /* quickly skip entry if wrong hash val */

    1639              :                 /*

    1647              : 
    1648              :                 /*

    1650              :                  * cache's list-of-lists, to speed subsequent searches.  (We do not

    1669         1784 :                 return cl;

    1671              : 
    1672              :         /*
    1673              :          * List was not found in cache, so we have to build it by reading the
    1674              :          * relation.  For each matching tuple found in the relation, use an

    1676              :          *

    1678              :          * get dropped from the cache while loading other members. We use a PG_TRY

    1686              : 
    1687              :         /*
    1688              :          * Cache invalidation can happen while we're building the list.

    1691              :          * that should be part of the list we're building.  Register an

    1709          417 :                 relation = table_open(cache->cc_reloid, AccessShareLock);

    1711              :                 /*

    1906              :  *      ReleaseCatCacheList
    1907              :  *

    1910              : void

    1928              : /*
    1929              :  * CatalogCacheCreateEntry
    1930              :  *              Create a new CatCTup entry, copying the given HeapTuple and other
    1931              :  *              supplied data into it.  The new entry initially has refcount 0.
    1932              :  *
    1933              :  * To create a normal cache entry, ntp must be the HeapTuple just fetched
    1934              :  * from scandesc, and "arguments" is not used.  To create a negative cache
    1935              :  * entry, pass NULL for ntp; then "arguments" is the cache keys to use.
    1936              :  * In either case, hashValue/hashIndex are the hash values computed from
    1937              :  * the cache keys.
    1938              :  *
    1939              :  * Returns NULL if we attempt to detoast the tuple and observe that it
    1940              :  * became stale.  (This cannot happen for a negative entry.)  Caller must
    1941              :  * retry the tuple lookup in that case.
    1942              :  */
    1943              : static CatCTup *
    1944        11203 : CatalogCacheCreateEntry(CatCache *cache, HeapTuple ntp, Datum *arguments,
    1945              :                                                 uint32 hashValue, Index hashIndex)
    1946              : {
    1947              :         CatCTup    *ct;
    1948              :         MemoryContext oldcxt;
    1949              : 
    1950        11203 :         if (ntp)
    1951              :         {
    1952              :                 int                     i;
    1953         7405 :                 HeapTuple       dtp = NULL;
    1954              : 
    1955              :                 /*
    1956              :                  * The invalidation of the in-progress entry essentially never happens
    1957              :                  * during our regression tests, and there's no easy way to force it to
    1958              :                  * fail for testing purposes.  To ensure we have test coverage for the
    1959              :                  * retry paths in our callers, make debug builds randomly fail about
    1960              :                  * 0.1% of the times through this code path, even when there's no
    1961              :                  * toasted fields.
    1962              :                  */
    1963              : #ifdef USE_ASSERT_CHECKING
    1964              :                 if (random() <= (MAX_RANDOM_VALUE / 1000))
    1965              :                         return NULL;
    1966              : #endif
    1967              : 
    1968              :                 /*
    1969              :                  * If there are any out-of-line toasted fields in the tuple, expand
    1970              :                  * them in-line.  This saves cycles during later use of the catcache
    1971              :                  * entry, and also protects us against the possibility of the toast
    1972              :                  * tuples being freed before we attempt to fetch them, in case of
    1973              :                  * something using a slightly stale catcache entry.
    1974              :                  */
    1975         7405 :                 if (HeapTupleHasExternal(ntp))
    1976              :                 {
    1977              :                         CatCInProgress *save_in_progress;
    1978              :                         CatCInProgress in_progress_ent;
    1979              : 
    1980              :                         /*
    1981              :                          * The tuple could become stale while we are doing toast table
    1982              :                          * access (since AcceptInvalidationMessages can run then).  The
    1983              :                          * invalidation will mark our in-progress entry as dead.
    1984              :                          */
    1985            0 :                         save_in_progress = catcache_in_progress_stack;
    1986            0 :                         in_progress_ent.next = catcache_in_progress_stack;
    1987            0 :                         in_progress_ent.cache = cache;
    1988            0 :                         in_progress_ent.hash_value = hashValue;
    1989            0 :                         in_progress_ent.list = false;
    1990            0 :                         in_progress_ent.dead = false;
    1991            0 :                         catcache_in_progress_stack = &in_progress_ent;
    1992              : 
    1993            0 :                         PG_TRY();
    1994              :                         {
    1995            0 :                                 dtp = toast_flatten_tuple(ntp, cache->cc_tupdesc);
    1996              :                         }
    1997            0 :                         PG_FINALLY();
    1998              :                         {
    1999              :                                 Assert(catcache_in_progress_stack == &in_progress_ent);
    2000            0 :                                 catcache_in_progress_stack = save_in_progress;
    2001              :                         }
    2002            0 :                         PG_END_TRY();
    2003              : 
    2004            0 :                         if (in_progress_ent.dead)
    2005              :                         {
    2006            0 :                                 heap_freetuple(dtp);
    2007            0 :                                 return NULL;
    2008              :                         }
    2009              :                 }
    2010              :                 else
    2011         7405 :                         dtp = ntp;
    2012              : 
    2013              :                 /* Allocate memory for CatCTup and the cached tuple in one go */
    2014         7405 :                 oldcxt = MemoryContextSwitchTo(CacheMemoryContext);
    2015              : 
    2016        14810 :                 ct = (CatCTup *) palloc(sizeof(CatCTup) +
    2017         7405 :                                                                 MAXIMUM_ALIGNOF + dtp->t_len);
    2018         7405 :                 ct->tuple.t_len = dtp->t_len;
    2019         7405 :                 ct->tuple.t_self = dtp->t_self;
    2020         7405 :                 ct->tuple.t_tableOid = dtp->t_tableOid;
    2021         7405 :                 ct->tuple.t_data = (HeapTupleHeader)
    2022         7405 :                         MAXALIGN(((char *) ct) + sizeof(CatCTup));
    2023              :                 /* copy tuple contents */

    2025         7405 :                            (const char *) dtp->t_data,
    2026         7405 :                            dtp->t_len);
    2027         7405 :                 MemoryContextSwitchTo(oldcxt);
    2028              : 
    2029         7405 :                 if (dtp != ntp)
    2030            0 :                         heap_freetuple(dtp);
    2031              : 
    2032              :                 /* extract keys - they'll point into the tuple if not by-value */
    2033        20271 :                 for (i = 0; i < cache->cc_nkeys; i++)
    2034              :                 {
    2035              :                         Datum           atp;
    2036              :                         bool            isnull;
    2037              : 
    2038        12866 :                         atp = heap_getattr(&ct->tuple,

    2040              :                                                            cache->cc_tupdesc,
    2041              :                                                            &isnull);
    2042              :                         Assert(!isnull);

    2044              :                 }
    2045              :         }

    2047              :         {
    2048              :                 /* Set up keys for a negative cache entry */
    2049         3798 :                 oldcxt = MemoryContextSwitchTo(CacheMemoryContext);
    2050         3798 :                 ct = (CatCTup *) palloc(sizeof(CatCTup));
    2051              : 
    2052              :                 /*
    2053              :                  * Store keys - they'll point into separately allocated memory if not

    2055              :                  */
    2056         3798 :                 CatCacheCopyKeys(cache->cc_tupdesc, cache->cc_nkeys, cache->cc_keyno,
    2057         3798 :                                                  arguments, ct->keys);
    2058         3798 :                 MemoryContextSwitchTo(oldcxt);
    2059              :         }
    2060              : 
    2061              :         /*
    2062              :          * Finish initializing the CatCTup header, and add it to the cache's

    2065        11203 :         ct->ct_magic = CT_MAGIC;
    2066        11203 :         ct->my_cache = cache;
    2067        11203 :         ct->c_list = NULL;
    2068        11203 :         ct->refcount = 0;                    /* for the moment */
    2069        11203 :         ct->dead = false;
    2070        11203 :         ct->negative = (ntp == NULL);
    2071        11203 :         ct->hash_value = hashValue;
    2072              : 
    2073        11203 :         dlist_push_head(&cache->cc_bucket[hashIndex], &ct->cache_elem);
    2074              : 
    2075        11203 :         cache->cc_ntup++;
    2076        11203 :         CacheHdr->ch_ntup++;
    2077              : 
    2078              :         /*
    2079              :          * If the hash table has become too full, enlarge the buckets array. Quite
    2080              :          * arbitrarily, we enlarge when fill factor > 2.
    2081              :          */
    2082        11203 :         if (cache->cc_ntup > cache->cc_nbuckets * 2)
    2083           20 :                 RehashCatCache(cache);
    2084              : 
    2085        11203 :         return ct;
    2086              : }

    2088              : /*
    2089              :  * Helper routine that frees keys stored in the keys array.
    2090              :  */
    2091              : static void
    2092         2014 : CatCacheFreeKeys(TupleDesc tupdesc, int nkeys, int *attnos, Datum *keys)
    2093              : {
    2094              :         int                     i;
    2095              : 
    2096         6221 :         for (i = 0; i < nkeys; i++)
    2097              :         {
    2098         4207 :                 int                     attnum = attnos[i];
    2099              :                 Form_pg_attribute att;
    2100              : 
    2101              :                 /* system attribute are not supported in caches */
    2102              :                 Assert(attnum > 0);
    2103              : 
    2104         4207 :                 att = TupleDescAttr(tupdesc, attnum - 1);
    2105              : 
    2106         4207 :                 if (!att->attbyval)
    2107         1599 :                         pfree(DatumGetPointer(keys[i]));
    2108              :         }
    2109         2014 : }
    2110              : 
    2111              : /*
    2112              :  * Helper routine that copies the keys in the srckeys array into the dstkeys
    2113              :  * one, guaranteeing that the datums are fully allocated in the current memory
    2114              :  * context.
    2115              :  */
    2116              : static void
    2117         4215 : CatCacheCopyKeys(TupleDesc tupdesc, int nkeys, int *attnos,
    2118              :                                  Datum *srckeys, Datum *dstkeys)
    2119              : {
    2120              :         int                     i;
    2121              : 
    2122              :         /*
    2123              :          * XXX: memory and lookup performance could possibly be improved by
    2124              :          * storing all keys in one allocation.
    2125              :          */
    2126              : 
    2127        13199 :         for (i = 0; i < nkeys; i++)
    2128              :         {

    2132              :                 NameData        srcname;

    2135              :                  * Must be careful in case the caller passed a C string where a NAME

    2137              :                  * Otherwise the memcpy() done by datumCopy() could fall off the end
    2138              :                  * of memory.

    2152              : 
    2153              : /*
    2154              :  *      PrepareToInvalidateCacheTuple()
    2155              :  *
    2156              :  *      This is part of a rather subtle chain of events, so pay attention:
    2157              :  *
    2158              :  *      When a tuple is inserted or deleted, it cannot be flushed from the
    2159              :  *      catcaches immediately, for reasons explained at the top of cache/inval.c.
    2160              :  *      Instead we have to add entry(s) for the tuple to a list of pending tuple
    2161              :  *      invalidations that will be done at the end of the command or transaction.
    2162              :  *
    2163              :  *      The lists of tuples that need to be flushed are kept by inval.c.  This
    2164              :  *      routine is a helper routine for inval.c.  Given a tuple belonging to
    2165              :  *      the specified relation, find all catcaches it could be in, compute the
    2166              :  *      correct hash value for each such catcache, and call the specified
    2167              :  *      function to record the cache id and hash value in inval.c's lists.
    2168              :  *      SysCacheInvalidate will be called later, if appropriate,
    2169              :  *      using the recorded information.
    2170              :  *
    2171              :  *      For an insert or delete, tuple is the target tuple and newtuple is NULL.
    2172              :  *      For an update, we are called just once, with tuple being the old tuple
    2173              :  *      version and newtuple the new version.  We should make two list entries
    2174              :  *      if the tuple's hash value changed, but only one if it didn't.
    2175              :  *
    2176              :  *      Note that it is irrelevant whether the given tuple is actually loaded
    2177              :  *      into the catcache at the moment.  Even if it's not there now, it might
    2178              :  *      be by the end of the command, or there might be a matching negative entry
    2179              :  *      to flush --- or other backends' caches might have such entries --- so
    2180              :  *      we have to make list entries to flush it later.
    2181              :  *
    2182              :  *      Also note that it's not an error if there are no catcaches for the
    2183              :  *      specified relation.  inval.c doesn't know exactly which rels have
    2184              :  *      catcaches --- it will call this routine for any tuple that's in a
    2185              :  *      system relation.
    2186              :  */
    2187              : void
    2188        10582 : PrepareToInvalidateCacheTuple(Relation relation,
    2189              :                                                           HeapTuple tuple,
    2190              :                                                           HeapTuple newtuple,
    2191              :                                                           void (*function) (int, uint32, Oid))
    2192              : {
    2193              :         slist_iter      iter;
    2194              :         Oid                     reloid;
    2195              : 
    2196              :         CACHE_elog(DEBUG2, "PrepareToInvalidateCacheTuple: called");
    2197              : 
    2198              :         /*
    2199              :          * sanity checks
    2200              :          */
    2201              :         Assert(RelationIsValid(relation));
    2202              :         Assert(HeapTupleIsValid(tuple));
    2203              :         Assert(PointerIsValid(function));
    2204              :         Assert(CacheHdr != NULL);
    2205              : 
    2206        10582 :         reloid = RelationGetRelid(relation);
    2207              : 
    2208              :         /* ----------------
    2209              :          *      for each cache
    2210              :          *         if the cache contains tuples from the specified relation
    2211              :          *                 compute the tuple's hash value(s) in this cache,
    2212              :          *                 and call the passed function to register the information.
    2213              :          * ----------------
    2214              :          */
    2215              : 
    2216       835978 :         slist_foreach(iter, &CacheHdr->ch_caches)
    2217              :         {
    2218       825396 :                 CatCache   *ccp = slist_container(CatCache, cc_next, iter.cur);
    2219              :                 uint32          hashvalue;
    2220              :                 Oid                     dbid;
    2221              : 
    2222       825396 :                 if (ccp->cc_reloid != reloid)
    2223       806893 :                         continue;
    2224              : 
    2225              :                 /* Just in case cache hasn't finished initialization yet... */
    2226        18503 :                 if (ccp->cc_tupdesc == NULL)
    2227            0 :                         CatalogCacheInitializeCache(ccp);
    2228              : 
    2229        18503 :                 hashvalue = CatalogCacheComputeTupleHashValue(ccp, ccp->cc_nkeys, tuple);
    2230        18503 :                 dbid = ccp->cc_relisshared ? (Oid) 0 : MyDatabaseId;
    2231              : 
    2232        18503 :                 (*function) (ccp->id, hashvalue, dbid);
    2233              : 
    2234        18503 :                 if (newtuple)
    2235              :                 {
    2236              :                         uint32          newhashvalue;
    2237              : 
    2238         1420 :                         newhashvalue = CatalogCacheComputeTupleHashValue(ccp, ccp->cc_nkeys, newtuple);
    2239              : 
    2240         1420 :                         if (newhashvalue != hashvalue)
    2241            6 :                                 (*function) (ccp->id, newhashvalue, dbid);
    2242              :                 }
    2243              :         }
    2244        10582 : }
    2245              : 
    2246              : 
    2247              : /*
    2248              :  * Subroutines for warning about reference leaks.  These are exported so
    2249              :  * that resowner.c can call them.
    2250              :  */
    2251              : void
    2252            0 : PrintCatCacheLeakWarning(HeapTuple tuple)
    2253              : {
    2254            0 :         CatCTup    *ct = (CatCTup *) (((char *) tuple) -
    2255              :                                                                   offsetof(CatCTup, tuple));
    2256              : 
    2257              :         /* Safety check to ensure we were handed a cache entry */
    2258              :         Assert(ct->ct_magic == CT_MAGIC);
    2259              : 
    2260            0 :         elog(WARNING, "cache reference leak: cache %s (%d), tuple %u/%u has count %d",
    2261              :                  ct->my_cache->cc_relname, ct->my_cache->id,
    2262              :                  ItemPointerGetBlockNumber(&(tuple->t_self)),
    2263              :                  ItemPointerGetOffsetNumber(&(tuple->t_self)),
    2264              :                  ct->refcount);
    2265            0 : }
    2266              : 
    2267              : void
    2268            0 : PrintCatCacheListLeakWarning(CatCList *list)
    2269              : {
    2270            0 :         elog(WARNING, "cache reference leak: cache %s (%d), list %p has count %d",
    2271              :                  list->my_cache->cc_relname, list->my_cache->id,
